installStep.name: MCM — Boss Readiness, Mastery Tracker, and Feature Toggles

plugin.name: Include MCM (SkyUI required — recommended)
plugin.description:
Adds a three-page in-game MCM menu.
• Boss Readiness — Multi-vector threat assessment for Alduin Phase 1 (Throat of the World), Alduin Final (Sovngarde), Harkon, and Miraak. Drain Vitality is UNRESISTABLE — the panel shows how many 25-HP pulses your current HP pool survives. Thresholds raise automatically when "World Eater's Influence" or "Immersive Miraak Difficulty" are detected.
• Mastery — Live view of all 13 mastery skills: current level (0-100) and bonus percentage (0-50%) for each.
• Features — Live toggles for every MCM-controllable feature: Resistance cap removal, Armor cap, Elemental Absorb, Carry Weight bonus, Arrow Recovery, 3-Day Cell Reset, Out-of-Combat Regen, and the Mastery System itself. Changes apply immediately — no restart required.
All toggles set GlobalVariables in MRO.esp and call Game.SetGameSettingFloat at runtime. Requires SkyUI 5.2+ and SKSE64.

plugin.name: No MCM (skip)
plugin.description: Skip the MCM. All gameplay systems (absorb, mastery bonuses, vendor tweaks, boss readiness) still function. Features default to ON and cannot be toggled without an MCM.

plugin.name: Enable Mastery System (recommended)
plugin.description:
Adds 13 mastery skills tracked via Custom Skills Framework (CSF). Mastery unlocks once a vanilla skill reaches 100 and advances through natural usage — swings, spells fired, bows drawn, crafting sessions, time spent in combat wearing armor.
Each mastery level (0 to 100) earns a scaling bonus: • One-Handed / Two-Handed / Archery: up to +50% attack damage • Light Armor / Heavy Armor: up to +50 Damage Resist • Destruction / Restoration / Alteration / Conjuration / Illusion: up to +50 to the school's effective skill level • Smithing / Alchemy / Enchanting: up to +25% crafting bonus
XP curve: each mastery level requires ~2x the effort of going from skill 99 to 100 in vanilla Skyrim, extrapolated through level 200. The difficulty per level grows as mastery deepens.
Can be toggled on/off in-game via the Features MCM page. Requires Custom Skills Framework (already in most Requiem lists).

plugin.name: Disable Mastery System
plugin.description: Skip the mastery system entirely. No CSF skill files are installed. All other MRO features (absorb, vendor tweaks, boss readiness MCM, early game buff) remain active.

installStep.name: Experience XP Rebalancing (if Experience.esp is installed)

plugin.name: Install MRO FF-style Experience.ini (recommended if using Experience.esp)
plugin.description:
Installs a rebalanced Experience.ini tuned for a Final Fantasy-style XP feel at the same total leveling speed as the "Faster Leveling" optional.
Changes from default LoreRim Experience settings: • Kill XP: same rate as Faster Leveling (fKillingMult=2.0) • Group bonus: +20% per additional enemy (FF "encounter" feel) • Boss bonus: higher XP for fighting above your level (fMaxLevelMult=5.0) • Dungeon clears: 200-400 XP (50-100% more than base LoreRim) • Main quest milestones: 350-400 XP (narrative rewards) • Discovery: 20 XP (same as Faster Leveling — exploration unchanged)
Only install if Experience.esp is in your load order and you want a kills-and-quests focused XP curve. Skip if you use a different XP mod.

plugin.name: Skip (no Experience.esp, or keep existing settings)
plugin.description: Do not install an Experience.ini. Your existing XP settings are unchanged. Vanilla Skyrim skill XP is also unmodified by MRO — we do not touch fSkillUseMult or similar GMSTs.
